title: rhinanthus borealis subsp. kyrollae
intro: Rhinanthus borealis subsp. kyrollae, also known as the Alpenrose or Kyrolla's Roridula, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the broomrape family, Orobanchaceae. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional European herbalism, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. Rhinanthus borealis subsp. kyrollae is believed to offer various health benefits, including the potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and soothe respiratory problems. The plant is typically prepared as a tea or tincture, and in some cases, it is used in topical applications, such as salves or creams, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
